header.title: EchoLog
header.short: Multi-tenant customer feedback SaaS — boards, upvoting, comments, workspace management. Full-stack TypeScript with React 19, Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL.

summary.heading: Summary
summary.text: EchoLog is a multi-tenant customer feedback SaaS — a Canny.io alternative — that allows product teams to collect, organize, and act on user feedback through boards, upvoting, comments, and workspace management.

problem.heading: The problem
problem.text: Product teams need a centralized place to gather user feedback, prioritize features based on community votes, and communicate status updates — without building it from scratch or paying for expensive tools like Canny. Most alternatives are either overpriced, locked into proprietary ecosystems, or lack essential features like role-based access and multi-workspace isolation.

solution.heading: The Solution
list: Built a full-stack monorepo with pnpm workspaces: React 19 frontend, Express API backend, and shared Zod contracts for end-to-end type safety.
Architected the backend with screaming architecture — business domains (auth, boards, posts, votes, comments, workspaces, invitations, notifications) as top-level folders.
Implemented JWT authentication in httpOnly cookies with cross-origin support for Vercel + Fly.io deployment, including mobile auth fix via API proxy.
Designed multi-tenant isolation using composite unique keys (`@@unique([workspaceId, slug])`) and role-based access (OWNER, ADMIN, MEMBER, VIEWER) with membership middleware on every endpoint.
Delivered comprehensive testing: 75+ tests across Vitest (unit + integration), React Testing Library (component), and Playwright (E2E), plus GitHub Actions CI.

architecture.heading: Architecture & decisions
architecture.text: EchoLog follows screaming architecture on the backend — each business domain is a top-level folder that 'screams' its purpose without revealing the framework. The frontend uses feature-based organization with React Query for server state and Zustand for UI state. Shared Zod contracts ensure type safety across the stack.
list: Screaming architecture: server/src/auth/, boards/, comments/, posts/, votes/, workspaces/, invitations/, notifications/ — each with controller, service, and router layers.
Shared Zod contracts in `shared/contracts/` serve as single source of truth for frontend forms AND backend validation — derived DTO types via z.infer.
Multi-tenant isolation via composite Prisma keys: `@@unique([workspaceId, slug])` ensures slugs are unique per workspace, not globally.
JWT stored in httpOnly cookies with sameSite=none for cross-origin production (Vercel → Fly.io) — no localStorage, XSS-resistant.
Feature branch workflow with conventional commits, atomic commits, and PRs — every change documented via Spec-Driven Development.

implementation.heading: Featured implementation
implementation.hookDescription: A reusable Express middleware that extracts the workspace ID from route params, body, or query, looks up the user's workspace membership in a single Prisma query, and optionally enforces a minimum role. This middleware protects every workspace-scoped endpoint in the application — boards, posts, votes, comments, invitations, and settings — ensuring consistent authorization across the entire backend.

accessibility.heading: Accessibility
accessibility.lead: EchoLog was audited systematically (P0→P3) for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across all interfaces.
list: Skip link, semantic landmarks, and proper heading hierarchy throughout the application.
Form errors announced via aria-live regions, input labels with htmlFor, and aria-describedby for helper text.
Keyboard navigation preserved across all interactive components: boards, posts, votes, comments, and modals.
Dynamic page titles updated on route changes for screen reader context.
Focus management in dialogs (trap + restore), mobile sidebar (auto-focus + Escape), and interactive elements.
prefers-reduced-motion respected — all framer-motion and CSS animations respect the system setting.

performance.heading: Performance & results
list: List virtualization with @tanstack/react-virtual gated on mobile OR >20 items — large boards scroll at 60fps even with hundreds of posts.
React Query optimistic updates on votes — instant UI feedback with automatic rollback on 409 conflicts.
Debounced search (300ms) prevents excessive API calls during fast typing.
Cursor-based pagination with 'Load more' pattern instead of offset/limit — consistent performance as the dataset grows.
Conditional rendering patterns: skeletons during loading, empty states with CTAs, and error boundaries with retry.

challenges.heading: Challenges & learnings
list: Cross-origin authentication on mobile: Vercel (frontend) and Fly.io (backend) are different origins. httpOnly cookies with sameSite=none fixed desktop but iOS Safari blocks third-party cookies. Solved with a Vite/Vercel API proxy rewrite.

Multi-tenant isolation without leaks: every Prisma query had to include workspaceId scoping. The membership middleware became the single source of truth for authorization, catching several potential data leaks during development.
Keeping Zod contracts truly shared between frontend and backend required careful dependency management in the monorepo — shared/ cannot import server or web code.
Testing real PostgreSQL in CI: switched from SQLite to a PostgreSQL service container in GitHub Actions for production parity, catching several SQL-dialect-specific bugs.

improvements.heading: What could be improved
list: Add real-time updates via WebSockets (Socket.IO or Server-Sent Events) for live vote counts and notifications.
Implement email service (Resend) for invitation emails, password reset, and notification digests.
Add public workspace discovery feed with popularity ranking and 'Continue without account' option.
Production hardening: rate limiting, structured logging (pino), and expanded test coverage for edge cases.
